name: controld-blade-mcp
title: Control-D — DNS Filtering & Privacy
description: Control-D DNS management — profiles, content filters, service routing, custom rules, device endpoints, and analytics. 22 tools with write gating and credential scrubbing.
tagline: DNS filtering and privacy from the conversation
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Profiles — create, configure, and clone DNS filtering profiles
Filtering — native and external blocklists, per-profile toggle
Services — block, bypass, spoof, or redirect 300+ named services
Custom rules — hostname-level DNS rules with folder organisation
Devices — endpoint management with per-device profile assignment
Write-gated — reads always work, writes require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Control-D Blade gives your AI agent full operational control over a Control-D DNS filtering account — the privacy-first DNS service that blocks ads, trackers, malware, and lets you route traffic through global proxies on a per-service basis.

22 tools across 8 categories cover the operational surface: account info, DNS profiles, content filters (native and third-party blocklists), service-level rules (block/bypass/spoof/redirect for 300+ named services), custom hostname rules, device endpoint management, IP access control, and analytics configuration.

### Token efficiency

DNS APIs return deeply nested JSON with metadata fields irrelevant to agent workflows. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts only actionable fields — pipe-delimited output at ~20–30 tokens per entity vs 80–120 for raw JSON. The service catalog is cached in-memory (1hr TTL) to avoid redundant API calls.

### Safety model

All 22 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** (12 tools) work immediately — list profiles, inspect filters, search rules
- **Writes** (10 tools) require `CONTROLD_WRITE_ENABLED=true`
- **Destructive operations** (rule delete, IP deauthorize) require an additional `confirm=true` parameter

Credentials are scrubbed from all error messages before they reach the LLM context.

### Tool inventory

| Category | Read | Write | What you can do |
|----------|------|-------|-----------------|
| Info | 3 | — | Account status, caller IP, network PoPs |
| Profiles | 2 | 2 | List, inspect, create, update DNS profiles |
| Filters | 1 | 1 | Native + external blocklists, batch toggle |
| Services | 2 | 1 | Service catalog, active rules, set actions |
| Rules | 2 | 3 | Custom hostname rules with folders |
| Devices | 1 | 2 | Endpoints with profile assignment |
| Access | 1 | 1 | IP authorisation on device endpoints |
| Analytics | 1 | — | Log levels and storage regions |

setup.blurb: DNS filtering and privacy — block ads, trackers, and malware with per-device policies and global service routing.
setup.help_text: Provide your Control-D API key. Get it from the Control-D dashboard under API settings. Enable write operations only if you want the agent to modify profiles, rules, and devices.

fields.label: API Key
fields.help: Control-D API token with appropriate permissions

fields.label: Enable Writes
fields.help: Allow the agent to create, update, and delete profiles, rules, and devices

scenarios.label: Audit DNS filtering coverage
scenarios.body: Pull all profiles with cd_profiles, check active filters and custom rules for each, review service-level blocks, and summarise protection gaps across devices.

scenarios.label: Block a domain across all profiles
scenarios.body: List profiles with cd_profiles, then create a custom BLOCK rule for the target hostname on each profile using cd_rule_create. Verify the rules are active with cd_rules.

scenarios.label: Route streaming through a proxy
scenarios.body: Search the service catalog with cd_service_catalog for the streaming service, then set a SPOOF rule with a proxy location using cd_service_update. Verify with cd_services.
